name: Consulting Client Accelerator
description: A complete client acquisition system for consultants and coaches — from outreach to signed contract

automation.action: Follow up in 48h if no response

assets.title: LinkedIn Outreach Sequence (3 Messages)
assets.content:
MESSAGE 1 — CONNECTION REQUEST
(300 character limit on LinkedIn)

"Hi [First Name] — I noticed [specific observation about their profile/content/company]. I work with [audience type] on [outcome]. Would love to connect and follow your work."

---
MESSAGE 2 — AFTER THEY ACCEPT (wait 1–2 days)

"Hey [First Name], thanks for connecting!

I noticed [something specific — their content, a recent post, their role, a company milestone].

[Genuine compliment or observation — 1 sentence]

I work with [their type of business] to [solve the problem they likely have] — typically helping them [specific measurable outcome].

I'm not going to pitch you — but I did put together a [free resource/tool/insight] specifically for [their role/industry] that might be useful: [LINK OR OFFER TO SEND IT]

No strings attached. Would you like me to send it over?

[Your Name]"

---
MESSAGE 3 — IF THEY ENGAGE WITH THE RESOURCE (3–5 days later)

"Hey [First Name],

Glad you found [resource] useful!

Based on [what you know about them], I think [specific thing you could help with] is probably on your radar.

I've helped [similar client type] tackle this — most recently [brief result].

Would you be open to a quick 20-minute call to see if there's a fit? No pressure — worst case you get a few ideas you can use right away.

Here's a link: [Calendly]

[Your Name]"

assets.title: Discovery Call Script
assets.content:
DISCOVERY CALL SCRIPT (20–30 minutes)

[0–2 MIN — WARM UP]
"Thanks for making time. Quick note — I have [20/30] minutes blocked for us. My goal today is to understand your situation and be honest about whether I can actually help. Sound good?"

[2–8 MIN — THEIR SITUATION]
• "Tell me about your business right now. What's going well?"
• "What made you want to hop on this call today specifically?"
• "How long have you been dealing with [their stated challenge]?"
• "What have you already tried to fix it?"

[8–15 MIN — DEEPER DIAGNOSIS]
• "What does success look like for you in the next 90 days? 6 months?"
• "What's the cost of NOT solving this? What happens if it stays as-is?"
• "Who else is involved in this decision?"
• "What's your timeline for making a change?"

[15–20 MIN — YOUR SOLUTION]
"Based on what you've shared — here's what I'm thinking."
[Describe your approach, NOT your features — connect it to what they said]
"The result most clients in your situation see is [specific outcome]."

[20–25 MIN — THE CLOSE]
"Does this sound like what you're looking for?"
[If yes:] "Here's what next steps look like..."
[If uncertain:] "What questions do you have?"
[If no:] "What's missing for you?"

[25–30 MIN — WRAP]
"What would need to be true for you to feel confident moving forward?"
[Address each concern directly]
"I'll send you a proposal/summary today. When can I expect to hear back?"

assets.title: Proposal Email Template
assets.content:
Subject: Your proposal — [Their Name] x [Your Name]

Hey [First Name],

Really enjoyed our conversation today. I can see clearly why [the problem] has been frustrating — and I'm confident there's a path through it.

Here's a summary of what we discussed and what I'm proposing:

---
WHAT I HEARD
• [Pain point 1 — their words, not yours]
• [Pain point 2]
• [Their goal in the timeframe they mentioned]

---
WHAT I'M PROPOSING
[PACKAGE NAME]: [1-sentence description]

Includes:
✅ [Deliverable 1]
✅ [Deliverable 2]
✅ [Deliverable 3]
✅ [Timeline for delivery]

---
OUTCOME
Based on your situation, here's what I expect you'll see:
• [Specific result 1]
• [Specific result 2]
• [Timeline]

---
INVESTMENT
[Package price]: $[AMOUNT]
[Payment options if applicable: full / 2 installments / monthly]

---
NEXT STEPS
If this feels right — reply to this email with "let's do it" and I'll send over the agreement and invoice.

If you have questions, I'm happy to jump on a quick call or answer by email.

I'd love to work together on this, [First Name]. I think [specific result they care most about] is very achievable.

[Your Name]

assets.title: Post-Proposal Follow-Up Sequence
assets.content:
FOLLOW-UP EMAIL 1 (48 hours after proposal)
Subject: Re: Your proposal — checking in

Hey [First Name],

Just following up on the proposal I sent — wanted to make sure it landed okay and see if any questions came up as you looked through it.

I also wanted to add one thing: [a relevant insight, a recent result from another client, or something that directly addresses their main concern from the call].

Happy to jump on a 10-minute call if that would help make the decision easier. Here's a link: [Calendly]

[Your Name]

---
FOLLOW-UP EMAIL 2 (5 days after proposal)
Subject: Still thinking it over?

Hey [First Name],

I know decisions like this take time — especially when you're already juggling everything else.

I want to be direct: I think [the thing they told you they want] is very achievable with the right help, and I believe I can deliver that for you.

Here's what [a recent client] said after working with me on something similar:
"[SHORT TESTIMONIAL — specific and relevant]"
— [Name, role]

If you're still on the fence, I'd love to understand what's holding you back. Reply and tell me — I'll give you an honest answer.

[Your Name]

---
FOLLOW-UP EMAIL 3 (10 days after proposal)
Subject: Closing the loop on this

Hey [First Name],

I've followed up a couple of times without hearing back — so I'm going to close the loop on this.

If the timing or fit isn't right, I completely understand. No hard feelings.

If circumstances change and you want to revisit — I'm here.

[Your Name]

assets.title: Objection Handling Scripts
assets.content:
OBJECTION HANDLING SCRIPTS
(Use during discovery calls or in follow-up emails)

---
OBJECTION: "It's too expensive."

Response: "I hear you on the investment. Let me ask — what would it be worth to you if [specific outcome they told you they want] happened in the next 90 days? [Pause for answer] Most of my clients see [specific ROI or result]. Does the investment feel different when you look at it that way?"

Alternative: "I also offer a [smaller engagement / payment plan / phased approach] if that works better for where you are right now. Want me to put together an alternative?"

---
OBJECTION: "I need to think about it."

Response: "Of course — what specifically do you need to think through? I'd rather address it now while we're both here than have you sit with an unanswered question."

---
OBJECTION: "I need to talk to my [partner/team/spouse]."

Response: "That makes complete sense. What would be helpful — should I put together a summary you can share with them? Or would it be easier to schedule a quick call with both of you?"

---
OBJECTION: "I've tried this before and it didn't work."

Response: "Thank you for sharing that — it's important context. Can you tell me more about what you tried? [Listen fully] Based on what you described, what didn't work was [X]. The approach I use is different in [specific way]. Here's why that matters for your situation: [explain]."

---
OBJECTION: "I'm not sure you understand my industry."

Response: "That's fair to question. Here's what I know about [their industry]: [2–3 specific things]. What I bring is [your specific expertise or framework] — which has worked across [industries]. But tell me: what's the specific thing about your industry that feels unique? Let's dig into it."

assets.title: Client Onboarding Email
assets.content:
Subject: Welcome — here's everything you need to get started

Hey [First Name],

Welcome aboard — I'm genuinely excited to work with you on this.

Here's everything you need to know for a smooth start:

---
IMPORTANT LINKS
• [Project management tool / client portal]: [LINK]
• Shared folder (documents, deliverables): [LINK]
• Schedule a call anytime: [Calendly link]

---
OUR FIRST STEPS
1. [Action 1 — e.g., complete the onboarding questionnaire: LINK]
2. [Action 2 — e.g., our kickoff call is scheduled for DATE]
3. [Action 3 — e.g., I'll send you the first deliverable by DATE]

---
HOW I WORK
• Communication: I check email [frequency] and typically respond within [timeframe]
• Updates: You'll get a [weekly/bi-weekly] progress update from me
• Questions: Anytime — email me at [your email]

---
WHAT TO EXPECT IN WEEK 1
[Brief description of what you'll be doing and what they'll see]

---
MY COMMITMENT TO YOU
You trusted me with [the thing they're trying to solve]. I don't take that lightly. My job is to [deliver specific result] — and I'll do everything I can to make sure you feel that.

Let's build something great.

[Your Name]
